data.title: Midland, MEH stay weak as high freight rates hurt export demand
data.description:
WTI Midland and WTI at East Houston, also known as MEH, remained weak on Friday, as high freight rates capped export demand.

WTI Midland traded at a 25-cent premium. It had touched its lowest in about seven months on Thursday. MEH traded at 95 cents, the lowest in over a month.

Coastal grades climbed as WTI's discount to Brent remained wide at minus $5.31 a barrel. A discount larger than $4 a barrel typically drives higher demand for barrels across the Atlantic, as traders spot an arbitrage window.

On the supply side, oil rigs held at 409 this week, Baker Hughes said.

  • Light Louisiana Sweet for March delivery eased 5 cents to a midpoint of a $2.25 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $2.00 and $2.50 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • Mars Sour rose 15 cents to trade at parity, and was seen bid and offered between a 20-cent premium and a 20-cent a barrel discount to U.S. crude futures

  • WTI Midland gained 10 cents to a midpoint of a 25-cent premium and was seen bid and offered between flat and a 50-cent a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • West Texas Sour rose 35 cents to a midpoint of a $3 discount and was seen bid and offered between a $3.25 and $3.75 a barrel discount to U.S. crude futures ​

  • WTI at East Houston, also known as MEH, traded between a 75-cent and $1.15 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • ICE Brent April futures rose 10 cents to settle at $71.76 a barrel

  • WTI March crude futures eased 4 cents to settle at $66.39 a barrel

  • The Brent/WTI spread narrowed 6 cents to last trade at minus $5.31, after hitting a high of minus $5.19 and a low of minus $5.32

data.title: Software Stocks Are Finally Stabilizing. They Could Bounce Back. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Jacob Sonenshine

Dare we say it is time to buy software shares? The stocks could rebound, at least in the short term, after a devastating slide.

The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector exchange-traded fund is down 31% to about $80 from a record high of just over $117 in late September. The reasons why are well known.

Anthropic and OpenAI could use their cutting-edge artificial intelligence technologies to do jobs now handled by specialized software, displacing some of the hundreds of companies that produce those programs. Meanwhile, Microsoft and Oracle are investing hundreds of billions of dollars annually in aggregate into AI data centers. But Oracle has borrowed a large portion of that, and many wonder whether Microsoft, like other software companies, will face a competitive threat from Anthropic and OpenAI.

Almost every stock in the software fund has gotten hammered. Over the course of the decline, many have argued that it is time to buy the dip, including Barron's . And every time that chorus grows louder, the stocks take another beating.

But now, the signals to buy are stronger.

The ETF hit a bottom early this month at just over $79, almost perfectly in line with where buyers have stepped in in the past.. The fund saw buying support at $81 after an April 2025 selloff, and in the high $70s to low $80s from late 2023 through 2024. That pattern shows traders and investors have been, and remain, extremely willing to buy at these cheaper prices, potentially sending the stocks higher.

The caveat is that the stocks could easily become cheaper. Savita Subramanian, Bank of America's head of U.S. equity and quantitative strategy, says her analysis, based on factors including recent changes to earnings estimates by analysts, indicates forward price/earnings multiples for software companies could drop some more.

That is certainly possible, especially if the major AI pioneers start offering business-specific AI products to corporate customers. That would prompt the market to assume estimates of near-term earnings for software will drop, sending the shares lower.

But those offerings of AI-based services are only just beginning to emerge. For the immediate term, the software group could experience a meaningful bounce.

An extremely encouraging sign: Insiders have bought shares. John Stanton, who has been on Microsoft's board of directors since 2014, bought 5,000 shares of the company late this month for close to $2 million, according to a Securities and Exchange Commission filing. That is the first insider Microsoft purchase in the past ten months, according to Jefferies trading analyst Jeff Favuzza.

Insider buying is a bullish signal because it indicates that someone who knows a company sees something in the fundamentals that could boost the stock soon.

Favuzza's research shows that since early 2022, while insiders have usually used rallies in Microsoft stock as opportunities to sell, the shares took off on the one occasion when insider buying emerged. The stock leapt 51% to a record high over the six months starting last April.

Even if software as we've known it has an uncertain future, the stocks will likely experience a major bounce. It could happen soon.

The market will continually assess the industry's future and will ultimately find those areas that are immune to the competitive threat. Cybersecurity is an example. The technology is highly complex, so it could be a long time until Anthropic and OpenAI develop the tech capabilities to take much market share from the current players in that business.

"Our judgment: if you want to add a Software name, add a fast-growing expensive one, like Palo Alto Networks, Fortinet, Synopsys," writes Adam Parker, founder of Trivariate Research. All those are cybersecurity stocks.

Any trader that wants large gains must take some risk. The risk is worth taking in software right now.

Write to Jacob Sonenshine at jacob.sonenshine@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Brazil's central bank to roll over swaps maturing in April
data.description:
Brazil's central bank said on Friday it will hold auctions starting on March 4 to roll over traditional currency swaps due on April 1.

  • The central bank usually seeks to provide currency hedging and maintain liquidity conditions for the market with its rolling policy.

  • In a traditional currency swap, the bond pays the buyer the currency variation plus an interest rate.

  • In return, the central bank receives the variation in the Brazilian benchmark interest rate, the Selic.

data.title: IMUX: Lead oral therapies show strong efficacy in MS and GI diseases, targeting multi-billion markets
data.description:
Vidofludimus calcium, a first-in-class oral therapy, shows strong efficacy and safety in both relapsing and progressive MS, targeting a $3-7B peak sales opportunity. IMU-856 demonstrated proof-of-concept in celiac disease and targets large GI and metabolic markets. Cash position is strengthened by a recent $200M raise.

Original document: Immunic, Inc. [IMUX] Slides Release — Feb. 20 2026

data.title: Signing Day Sports to merge into BlockchAIn with exchange ratio tied to last sale price
data.description:
Signing Day Sports announced terms for a business combination that will exchange its shares for BlockchAIn common shares using a price-linked ratio.

Key Highlights:

  • Signing Day Sports will merge into BlockchAIn as part of a multi-party business combination with One Blockchain.
  • Signing Day Sports stockholders will receive BlockchAIn common shares based on an Exchange Ratio tied to the last sale price divided by 7.5.
  • BlockchAIn may adjust the Exchange Ratio to preserve post-closing ownership proportions but Signing Day Sports holders must receive at least 8.5% of fully diluted shares.
  • The registration statement is effective and the definitive Proxy Statement/Prospectus has been distributed to Signing Day Sports stockholders.

Original SEC Filing: Signing Day Sports, Inc. [ SGN ] - 8-K - Feb. 20, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Exclusive: Opendoor CEO Reveals Company’s Own Mortgage Product Is In Beta Phase, Criticizes Fed On Interest Rates
data.description:
Key points:
  • In its bid to add more mortgage options to its platform, Opendoor acquired homebuyer.com, a mortgage data platform, in Dec. last year. 
  • OPEN’s CEO said that the company shipped its latest feature late Thursday. 
  • The real estate digital platform on Thursday posted revenue for the fourth-quarter (Q4) of $736 million, much above street expectations of $591.75 million, as per data from Fiscal.ai.

OpenDoor's (OPEN) Chief Executive Officer Kaz Nejatian in an exclusive interview to Stocktwits on Friday hinted that the company's mortgage product is in beta phase as the company pushes to enable itself to issue its own mortgage on homes it sells, helping cut out middlemen.

“I left the earnings call and came to the office and started working on our product because we actually shipped a new feature late last night around 11 p.m. So I was up a little late shipping,” Nejatian told Stocktwits’ in an interview. 

In its bid to add more mortgage options to its platform, Opendoor acquired homebuyer.com, a mortgage data platform, in Dec. last year. The deal fits OPEN’s CEO Kaz’s AI-pilled vision of using data to eliminate ‘toilful work’ like manual paperwork.

Critical Of Fed

Nejatian criticized the U.S. Federal Reserve for maintaining the interest rates at the level they are. 

“I think the Fed is being deeply irresponsible. Um, these days with maintaining these interest levels,” he told Stocktwits. 

“Trueflation, which is a much better measure of inflation than CPI, truflation has been sub 1 % basically the entire year. The Fed is asking people to believe that Bureau of Labor Statistics’ (BLS) data is a more reliable source for inflation and what's happening to the economy than truflation. And no one believes that. There's no one who invests their own money who would look at BLS data,” he added. 

The Federal Reserve kept the benchmark interest unchanged at 3.5%-3.75% in its January meeting. The latest Federal Open Market Committee’s (FOMC) minutes released by the Fed earlier this week revealed that the central bank might hike the interest rates if inflation does not come down to its targeted range. 

“I think it is highly irresponsible to have artificially elevated interest rates at this time. And I think they need to do something because this is just like, it's very odd,” Nejatian further added. 

Q4 Earnings

The real estate digital platform posted revenue for the fourth-quarter (Q4) of $736 million, much above street expectations of $591.75 million, as per data from Fiscal.ai. Meanwhile, the company’s loss per share came in at $1.26, trailing market expectations of a loss per share of $0.11.

Opendoor said that it expects Q1 revenue to decrease approximately 10% quarter-over-quarter.

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

Retail sentiment around OPEN trended in ‘extremely bullish’ territory amid ‘extremely high’ message volume. 

Shares in the company have risen 221% over the past year.

data.title: Verses AI Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Verses AI Inc., a leading innovator in agentic software platforms, has released its Q1 2026 10-Q report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial and operational performance. The report highlights the company's efforts in launching new products, expanding its market reach, and navigating financial challenges.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.82 million, reflecting an increase from the previous year, driven by the launch of the Genius platform.
  • Gross Profit: $0.13 million, indicating a positive margin despite high operating expenses.
  • Operating Income (Expense): $(24.90) million, showing a significant operating loss due to high selling, general, and administrative expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(24.98) million, primarily due to substantial operating expenses and other expenses.
  • Loss Per Class A Subordinate Voting Shares - Basic and Diluted: $(2.50), reflecting the impact of the net loss on shareholders.

Business Highlights

  • New Product Launches: In April 2025, Verses AI Inc. launched its flagship product, Genius, an innovative agentic software platform. Genius uses proprietary technology based on Active Inference to support and improve domain-specific decision-making by providing insights in volatile, uncertain, complex, or ambiguous data environments.
  • Revenue Segments: The company recognized revenue from contracts with customers, including Proof of Concept contracts and software implementation services, as well as Software as a Service (SaaS) offerings. Revenue was recognized both at a point in time and over the duration of contracts.
  • Operational Strategy: Verses AI Inc. has implemented a 'Lighthouse' strategy, focusing on licensing an enterprise version of Genius to financial institutions and asset managers managing over $100 million in assets. This strategy targets high-value entities such as pension funds, sovereign funds, insurance companies, and hedge funds.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to expand its target sectors beyond financial institutions to include robotics, logistics, and infrastructure management. This expansion is based on real-world results and feedback from initial target clients, which will help further develop and improve the Genius platform.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates primarily in the United States, with no additional operations or sources of revenue outside the country. The Chief Operating Decision Maker evaluates performance based on the company's net income as reported in the Statements of Operations.
  • Management Changes: On February 8, 2026, Gabriel Rene resigned as Chief Executive Officer and director. David Scott, a current board member, was appointed as interim CEO while a search for a permanent replacement is conducted. Dan Mapes, President Emeritus and Global Brand Ambassador, also resigned.
  • Business Model: Verses AI Inc. offers a range of pricing packages for Genius, including flat fee, profit share, and variable pricing options based on complexity and usage criteria such as the number of users, transactions, and input parameters.

SEC Filing: Verses AI Inc. [ VRSSF ] - 10-Q - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: Blue Owl Stock Rises After Saba Capital And Cox Capital’s Tender Offers For BDC Shares
data.description:
Key points:
  • Both the firms sent the notice to Blue Owl Capital Corporation II on February 17, 2026 of their intention to commence a tender offer to purchase a portion of outstanding shares of OBDC II in cash.
  • The tender offers would provide a liquidity solution to retail investors in the wake of a significant industry-wide increase in BDC redemption requests.
  • Blue Owl said on Thursday that it will permanently stop allowing redemptions from Blue Owl Capital Corp. II, a semi-liquid private credit fund marketed to U.S. retail investors.

Blue Owl Capital got a reprieve on Friday after Saba Capital and Cox Capital Partners announced cash tender offers for shares in business development companies (BDC) that are owned by Blue Owl. 

The two companies subsequently notified Blue Owl Technology Income Corp and Blue Owl Credit Income Corp of their intention to commence similar tender offers.

At the time of writing OWL stock was up 2.2% in extended hours of trading. 

Offer Details

The offer price is expected to be at a 20-35% discount to the most recent estimated net asset value and dividend reinvestment price. That will be determined when tender offers start after a 10-business day notice period for each BDC, Cox and Saba said.

Both the firms sent the notice to Blue Owl Capital Corporation II on February 17, 2026 of their intention to commence a tender offer to purchase a portion of outstanding shares of OBDC II in cash.

The tender offers would provide a liquidity solution to retail investors in the wake of a significant industry-wide increase in BDC redemption requests, multiple quarters of net outflows and a rise in redemption gate provisions.

Blue Owl’s Redemption Halt

Blue Owl said on Thursday that it will permanently stop allowing redemptions from Blue Owl Capital Corp. II, a semi-liquid private credit fund marketed to U.S. retail investors.

The announcement showed Blue Owl abandoning an earlier plan to reopen withdrawals later this quarter. Instead, the firm said it would begin returning investor capital.

Blue Owl said it was “not halting redemptions” but instead “changing the method” by which investors receive their cash. The firm said it remains well capitalized, with about $4 billion in total cash and borrowing capacity, and described its balance sheet as “well positioned” to support portfolio performance into 2026.

How Did Stocktwits’ Users React?

Retail sentiment around OWL trended in ‘extremely bullish’ territory amid ‘extremely high’ message volume. 

Shares in the company have fallen 54.5% over the past year.

data.title: Vir Biotechnology Inc expected to post a loss of 49 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Vir Biotechnology Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on February 23 for the period ending December 31 2025

  • The San Francisco California-based company is expected to report a 87.4% increase in revenue to $23.183 million from $12.37 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 6 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Vir Biotechnology Inc is for a loss of 49 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Vir Biotechnology Inc is $14.50, about 85.4% above its last closing price of $7.82
